question: How do Baker games affect team dynamics?
content:
1. Everyone shares the score
No one “owns” a full game.
Every frame depends on the teammate before and after you.

2. Momentum becomes a team thing
One strike lifts everyone.
One mistake affects everyone.
Emotions sync up fast.

3. Lineup order shapes the team
Each spot has a purpose:
Starter sets the tone
Middle players stabilize
Anchor handles pressure
People feel their role more clearly.

4. Communication increases
You must talk:
About lane changes
About adjustments
About confidence
Silence kills Baker performance.

5. Trust becomes essential
You hand the game to your teammates frame by frame.
Good trust = smooth flow.
Weak trust = tension and overthinking.
